description_long: This sculpture depicts a group of six life-sized, emaciated human figures standing and kneeling on a rectangular base. The figures are rendered in a highly expressive, textured style, with draped garments and anguished postures, some with outstretched arms or bowed heads. The surface is rough and patinated, giving the piece a dark, weathered bronze appearance. The figures are closely grouped, interacting with one another through their gestures and gazes, creating a sense of shared suffering or solemnity. The base is solid and slightly raised, supporting the weight and arrangement of the figures. The overall impression is one of historical or emotional gravity, with a strong sense of narrative conveyed through the figures' poses.
description: A dramatic bronze sculpture of six life-sized, anguished human figures in expressive poses, grouped closely on a rectangular base. The surface is rough and dark, emphasizing the emotional intensity and historical gravitas of the scene.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full group of six figures, with detailed faces and draped garments, some figures facing forward and others turned to the side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the kneeling figure in the foreground and the close clustering of the group, with more emphasis on the depth and overlapping forms.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the figures, revealing the textured drapery and the arrangement of the group from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the figures from a different angle, with the group’s arrangement and the base’s rectangular shape clearly visible.
attribute_location_context: The sculpture appears to be displayed on a solid base, suitable for outdoor public spaces such as plazas, parks, or urban squares.
